lines.content: So, the starting hourly rate is $20 an hour and then on top of that, our guys get tipped out on the majority of the moves. And that average is out to roughly about $7 extra dollars per hour of work. So for a full day, that average is out about like, $50 to sixty extra dollars on top of the hours worked.

lines.content: Yes, sir, that's awesome and I was gonna say like, so is it like, were you like, you know, you go into a job you clock in and you get paid hourly through that whole time or is it like only per job or like, say, you don't get paid as you're, like, going to that job no matter like, rather, it's like an hour or two hours away, but then once you start loading stuff into the truck, that's when you get paid or is it like just, yeah, like, you know, as soon as you clock in, you're getting paid until you clock out.
lines.name: John Ghacemi

lines.content: That one, as, as soon as you clock in, you're getting paid the full amount. We don't have two different like rates we pay our guys, it's just clocking whatever you're doing, you know, get, you get the truck rated to go in the morning or you're in route to the customer's home, or you know, going to the unload or whatever it is. It, it's the same, right. There's no, like, lower secondary rate.
